bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.title: Write-write data race: lobbyJoin mutates lobbyParams.GroupID and lobbyParams.Mode without synchronisation
bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.detail: lobbyJoin writes lobbyParams.GroupID = label.GetGroupID() and lobbyParams.Mode = label.Mode. When multiple concurrent goroutines call TryFollowPartyLeader with the same *LobbySessionParameters pointer (which lobbyFind does for party members), these writes race. The -race detector flags write-write races at evr_lobby_join.go:30 and :31. This is a new bug not covered by Grace's suite.
bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.fix: Either copy lobbyParams before passing to lobbyJoin, or make lobbyJoin take the group/mode parameters as separate arguments instead of mutating the shared struct.

bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.title: params.Mode field is not atomically protected; concurrent readers and the TryFollowPartyLeader mutator race
bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.detail: The LobbySessionParameters.Mode field is a plain evr.Symbol (uint64). TryFollowPartyLeader mutates it (SMELL H7: params.Mode = evr.ModeSocialPublic at line 1119) and lobbyJoin also writes it (line 31). lobbyFind reads it in a non-atomic switch at line 75. The race detector confirms concurrent goroutines racing on this field. Grace documented SMELL H7 as a side-effect issue; this test exposes the actual memory race.
bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.fix: Use atomic.Uint64 for LobbySessionParameters.Mode, or eliminate the mutation from TryFollowPartyLeader (SMELL H7 fix).

bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.title: TryFollowPartyLeader does not check ctx.Err() before calling lobbyJoin; does not respect pre-cancelled context
bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.detail: When the context is already cancelled before TryFollowPartyLeader reaches the lobbyJoin call, the function does not fast-path out. It proceeds into lobbyJoin → lobbyAuthorize → panics on nil metrics in the test environment. In production with real metrics, it would make unnecessary network calls with a cancelled context. The background goroutine in lobbyJoin uses time.After(3s) which blocks callers waiting for cleanup.
bugs_found_not_in_grace_suite.fix: Add 'if ctx.Err() != nil { return false }' immediately before the p.lobbyJoin call at evr_lobby_find.go:1137 and at the pollFollowPartyLeader call sites.

tests_that_pass.note: Symbol(0) correctly returns false. No panic.
ModeUnloaded not treated as social. Correct.
All 12 known mode constants produce the correct bool. Only failed when contaminated by CHAOS-1 races from parallel tests.
Arbitrary unknown Symbol does not match social. Correct.
200 concurrent goroutines reading shouldFollowerFindOrCreateSocial — no race. Correct.
nil ph guard in GetLeader works correctly.
nil ph guard in Size() works.
nil ph guard in List() works.
nil ph guard in ID() works.
nil ph guard in MatchmakerAdd returns error, not panic. Correct.
LobbyGroup.GetLeader() RLock/RUnlock and ph.Lock/Unlock correctly synchronise concurrent leader reads and writes. No race in the LobbyGroup itself.
Concurrent Size()/List() reads while members are joined — no race. PartyPresenceList is internally synchronised.
LobbyGroup with nil ph returns false from GetLeader; TryFollowPartyLeader handles it correctly.
uuid.Nil GroupID is a valid UUID for stream lookups; tracker does not panic.
Empty party list (Size()==0) doesn't cause panic in requiredSlots arithmetic; clamped to partySize=1.
255-member party doesn't overflow or panic in requiredSlots computation.
Concurrent leader clear during match lookup handled gracefully.
Stale match ID (not in registry) correctly returns false without panic.
pollFollowPartyLeader respects ctx.Done() at the top of its loop; returns false promptly.
nil ph LobbyGroup returns false from GetLeader; isLeaderHeadingToSocial handles it.
Cancelled context does not panic in isLeaderHeadingToSocial.
When session IS the leader, function returns false at the early guard.
All corrupt JSON inputs handled without panic. The SMELL is the silent fallthrough (not a crash), which is documented.
Concurrent tracker reads and writes during isLeaderHeadingToSocial — no race in the mock tracker's RWMutex.
Confirms SMELL C2 mechanistic path: nil bytes → empty Status → Unmarshal fails → wrong routing. Documents the bug.
nil entrants slice handled without panic.
All non-social modes return entrants unchanged.
No duplicate placeholders when all party members already in entrants slice.
Concurrent reads of lobbyGroup.List() during appendPartyReservationPlaceholders — no race.
!success branch in configureParty-style code logs warning and continues; no crash.
nil leader with non-nil expectedInitialLeader correctly returns the expected leader.
Empty party list returns 0, not negative.
Single-member party returns 1.

tests_that_fail_on_current_code.detail: lobbyJoin writes GroupID and Mode without synchronisation while concurrent TryFollowPartyLeader callers share the params pointer.
Same lobbyJoin write-write race, exposed by a combined Try+Poll concurrent scenario.
params.Mode is mutated by TryFollowPartyLeader (SMELL H7) while concurrent goroutines read it via shouldFollowerFindOrCreateSocial. Plain uint64 field has no atomic protection.

tests_that_fail_on_current_code.result: FAIL — DOES NOT RESPECT CANCELLED CONTEXT
tests_that_fail_on_current_code.detail: With pre-cancelled context, TryFollowPartyLeader does not fast-path out before calling lobbyJoin. In test environment this manifests as a panic from nil metrics inside lobbyAuthorize; in production it results in unnecessary calls on a cancelled context.

contamination_note: Several tests that PASS in isolation show as FAIL when run in the full parallel suite. This is because the data races in CHAOS-1/CHAOS-2 bleed across t.Parallel() test goroutines — the race detector reports the race in whichever test happens to be running when the racy goroutines from the failing tests execute. All non-contaminated tests verified clean in isolation runs.
grace_coverage_delta: Grace's suite covers: SMELL C1 (deferred Untrack TOCTOU), C2 (marshal failure silent discard), C3 (lobbyJoin nil return), H1-H11 (tracker isNew discards, duplication, mutations). The chaos suite adds: CHAOS-1 (write-write race in lobbyJoin on shared params), CHAOS-2 (unprotected params.Mode field race), CHAOS-3 (cancelled context not respected before lobbyJoin), plus exhaustive nil/zero/boundary/unknown-mode coverage for shouldFollowerFindOrCreateSocial, LobbyGroup nil-ph guards, appendPartyReservationPlaceholders edge cases, and corrupt JSON input robustness for isLeaderHeadingToSocial.
